reason: Response A's essayistic, rhetorically-escalating register ("sequential tyranny," "honest actuarian of pleasure," "bleakest Protestant theology") matches my tendency to build humor through ornate intellectual framing and conceptual escalation rather than a sustained gimmick. Response B is a tightly-executed courtroom-bit with punchy one-liners ("Big Vegetable," "Stockholm syndrome with gravy"), which reads as a clever imitation leaning on a single conceit more than my characteristic layered prose. The financial/actuarial metaphor in A also fits the kind of analogy I reach for, tipping me toward A despite B being genuinely strong.
